import ssl
import urllib.request
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches and extracts title and description metadata from a webpage.
    
    Prioritizes Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description) over standard HTML title
    and meta description tags. Uses secure SSL verification and includes timeout protection.
    
    Args:
        page_url: URL of the webpage to scrape
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary with keys 'title' and 'description' containing extracted strings
        (empty strings if no metadata found)
        
    Raises:
        Exceptions from urllib.request.urlopen (URLError, HTTPError, timeout, etc.)
        will propagate to caller for handling
    """
    # Create secure SSL context using certifi's CA bundle
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    # Fetch content with timeout protection (prevents resource exhaustion attacks)
    timeout = 10  # seconds
    with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=timeout) as response:
        # Determine charset from headers, default to UTF-8
        charset = response.headers.get_content_charset(failobj='utf-8')
        # Read and decode content, ignoring invalid byte sequences (prevents decode crashes)
        html_bytes = response.read()
        html_text = html_bytes.decode(charset, errors='ignore')
    
    # Custom parser to extract metadata with Open Graph priority
    class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.title = None          # Standard <title> content
            self.og_title = None       # Open Graph title
            self.og_description = None # Open Graph description
            self.meta_description = None # Standard meta description
            self.in_title = False
            self.title_data = []
        
        def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Process first <title> tag only
            if tag == 'title' and self.title is None:
                self.in_title = True
                self.title_data = []
            # Process meta tags for Open Graph and standard description
            elif tag == 'meta':
                # Open Graph title
                if not self.og_title and attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:title' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                    self.og_title = attrs_dict['content']
                # Open Graph description
                if not self.og_description and attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:description' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                    self.og_description = attrs_dict['content']
                # Standard meta description
                if not self.meta_description and attrs_dict.get('name') == 'description' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                    self.meta_description = attrs_dict['content']
        
        def handle_data(self, data):
            if self.in_title:
                self.title_data.append(data)
        
        def handle_endtag(self, tag):
            if tag == 'title' and self.in_title:
                self.in_title = False
                if self.title is None:  # Only set on first title closure
                    self.title = ''.join(self.title_data).strip()
    
    # Parse HTML and extract metadata
    parser = MetadataParser()
    parser.feed(html_text)
    parser.close()
    
    # Prioritize Open Graph values, fallback to standard tags, ensure clean strings
    title = (parser.og_title or parser.title or '').strip()
    description = (parser.og_description or parser.meta_description or '').strip()
    
    return {
        'title': title,
        'description': description
    }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme to prevent SSRF and invalid protocols
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Invalid URL',
                'description': 'Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are allowed'
            }

        # Prepare request with headers to avoid bot blocking
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0; +https://example.com/bot)'
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        # Create SSL context with certifi certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch content with timeout protection
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Determine encoding from headers with UTF-8 fallback
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract title (case-insensitive, handles newlines)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description robustly (handles attribute order variations)
        description = "No description available"
        meta_tags = re.findall(r'<meta[^>]*>', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        for tag in meta_tags:
            # Check if tag contains name="description" (case-insensitive, flexible spacing)
            if re.search(r'name\s*=\s*["\']description["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE):
                # Extract content attribute value (handles single/double quotes)
                content_match = re.search(r'content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE)
                if content_match:
                    description = content_match.group(1).strip()
                    break

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # Catch-all for network errors, parsing failures, timeouts, SSL issues, etc.
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate and parse URL
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Block non-HTTP/S schemes
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are allowed.")
        
        # Block URLs containing credentials
        if parsed_url.username is not None or parsed_url.password is not None:
            raise ValueError("URLs containing credentials are blocked for security reasons.")
        
        # Ensure hostname exists
        if not parsed_url.hostname:
            raise ValueError("URL must contain a valid hostname.")
        
        # Normalize hostname for comparison
        hostname_lower = parsed_url.hostname.lower()
        
        # Block known internal hostnames (exact matches)
        internal_hostnames = {'localhost', '127.0.0.1', '0.0.0.0', '::1'}
        if hostname_lower in internal_hostnames:
            raise ValueError("Access to internal hostnames is blocked.")
        
        # Block private/reserved IP addresses if hostname is an IP
        try:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(parsed_url.hostname)
            if (ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local or 
                ip.is_multicast or ip.is_unspecified):
                raise ValueError("Access to private/reserved IP addresses is blocked.")
        except ValueError:
            # Not a valid IP address string - treat as domain name
            pass
        
        # Block non-standard ports (only allow 80/443 when explicitly specified)
        port = parsed_url.port
        if port is not None and port not in (80, 443):
            raise ValueError("Non-standard ports are blocked. Only ports 80 and 443 are permitted.")
        
        # Fetch content with strict timeout and SSL validation
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (ValueError, urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Generic error response to avoid information leakage
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches HTML content from a URL and extracts page title and description metadata.
    
    Prioritizes Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description) for social media context,
    falling back to standard HTML title tag and meta description tag.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary with 'title' and 'description' keys containing extracted strings.
        Returns empty strings for missing values. Handles network/decoding errors gracefully.
    """
    # Create SSL context using certifi's certificate bundle for secure HTTPS requests
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            # Determine character encoding from headers (default to UTF-8)
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            html_bytes = response.read()
            html_content = html_bytes.decode(charset, errors='ignore')
    except Exception:
        # Return empty metadata on any fetch/decode failure per service robustness requirements
        return {'title': '', 'description': ''}
    
    class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.og_title = None
            self.og_description = None
            self.meta_description = None
            self.standard_title = None
            self.in_title = False
            self.title_parts = []
        
        def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
            tag_lower = tag.lower()
            attrs_dict = {k.lower(): v for k, v in attrs}
            
            # Capture first standard title tag content
            if tag_lower == 'title' and self.standard_title is None:
                self.in_title = True
                self.title_parts = []
            # Process meta tags for Open Graph and standard description
            elif tag_lower == 'meta':
                # Open Graph title
                if (self.og_title is None and 
                    attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:title' and 
                    'content' in attrs_dict):
                    self.og_title = attrs_dict['content']
                # Open Graph description
                if (self.og_description is None and 
                    attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:description' and 
                    'content' in attrs_dict):
                    self.og_description = attrs_dict['content']
                # Standard meta description
                if (self.meta_description is None and 
                    attrs_dict.get('name') == 'description' and 
                    'content' in attrs_dict):
                    self.meta_description = attrs_dict['content']
        
        def handle_data(self, data):
            if self.in_title:
                self.title_parts.append(data)
        
        def handle_endtag(self, tag):
            if tag.lower() == 'title' and self.in_title:
                self.standard_title = ''.join(self.title_parts).strip()
                self.in_title = False
    
    parser = MetadataParser()
    try:
        parser.feed(html_content)
    except Exception:
        # Continue with partial results if HTML parsing fails
        pass
    
    # Handle unclosed title tag by using collected content
    if parser.in_title and parser.standard_title is None:
        parser.standard_title = ''.join(parser.title_parts).strip()
    
    # Determine final values with Open Graph priority
    title = (parser.og_title or parser.standard_title or '').strip()
    description = (parser.og_description or parser.meta_description or '').strip()
    
    return {
        'title': title,
        'description': description
    }

import urllib.request
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re


# Create SSL context once at module level for efficiency and security
SSL_CONTEXT = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
REQUEST_TIMEOUT = 10  # seconds


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """Custom HTML parser to extract Open Graph and standard metadata."""
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.og_title = None
        self.og_description = None
        self.meta_description = None
        self.standard_title = None
        self.in_title = False
        self.title_data = []
    
    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
        if tag == 'meta':
            # Prioritize Open Graph tags (common in social media)
            if attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:title' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                self.og_title = attrs_dict['content']
            if attrs_dict.get('property') == 'og:description' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                self.og_description = attrs_dict['content']
            # Fallback to standard meta description
            if attrs_dict.get('name') == 'description' and 'content' in attrs_dict:
                self.meta_description = attrs_dict['content']
        elif tag == 'title':
            self.in_title = True
            self.title_data = []
    
    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self.in_title:
            self.title_data.append(data)
    
    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == 'title' and self.in_title:
            self.in_title = False
            self.standard_title = ''.join(self.title_data).strip()


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetch and extract title/description metadata from a webpage.
    
    Security measures implemented:
    - URL scheme validation to prevent SSRF (only http/https allowed)
    - SSL certificate verification using certifi CA bundle
    - Request timeout to prevent hanging
    - Comprehensive exception handling to avoid information leakage
    - Charset handling with fallback to prevent decoding crashes
    
    Args:
        page_url: Target webpage URL (must use http or https scheme)
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with 'title' and 'description' keys (values are str or None)
    """
    # Validate URL scheme to mitigate SSRF risks
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https') or not parsed.netloc:
            return {'title': None, 'description': None}
    except Exception:
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}
    
    try:
        # Fetch content with security controls
        with urllib.request.urlopen(
            page_url, 
            context=SSL_CONTEXT, 
            timeout=REQUEST_TIMEOUT
        ) as response:
            # Extract charset from Content-Type header with fallback
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            charset_match = re.search(r'charset=["\']?([^\s;"\']+)', content_type)
            charset = charset_match.group(1) if charset_match else 'utf-8'
            
            # Safely decode response content
            raw_html = response.read()
            try:
                html = raw_html.decode(charset, errors='strict')
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                html = raw_html.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
        
        # Parse metadata
        parser = MetadataParser()
        parser.feed(html)
        parser.close()
        
        # Prioritize Open Graph values over standard tags
        title = parser.og_title or parser.standard_title
        description = parser.og_description or parser.meta_description
        
        # Clean and normalize results
        return {
            'title': title.strip() if title and title.strip() else None,
            'description': description.strip() if description and description.strip() else None
        }
    
    except Exception:
        # Fail securely: never expose internal errors or partial data
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}

import urllib.request
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches HTML content from the given URL and extracts page title and description metadata.
    
    Uses certifi certificates for SSL verification and handles common metadata sources:
    - Title: Content of the first <title> tag
    - Description: Prioritizes Open Graph description (og:description) over standard meta description
    
    Args:
        page_url: Valid URL to a public webpage (e.g., 'https://example.com/article')
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with 'title' and 'description' keys containing extracted strings.
        Returns empty strings for missing values.
    
    Raises:
        Exceptions from urllib.request.urlopen (URLError, HTTPError, timeout, etc.) 
        will propagate to caller for handling.
    """
    # Create SSL context using certifi's certificate bundle for reliable HTTPS verification
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    # Fetch page content with timeout protection
    with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
        # Determine encoding from HTTP headers (default to UTF-8 if unspecified)
        encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
        html_bytes = response.read()
        html_text = html_bytes.decode(encoding, errors='ignore')
    
    # Custom HTML parser to extract metadata
    class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.title = None
            self.og_description = None
            self.meta_description = None
            self.in_title = False
            self.title_parts = []
        
        def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
            tag_lower = tag.lower()
            attrs_dict = {k.lower(): v for k, v in attrs}  # Normalize attribute names
            
            # Process title tag (only first occurrence)
            if tag_lower == 'title' and self.title is None:
                self.in_title = True
                self.title_parts = []
            
            # Process meta tags for descriptions
            elif tag_lower == 'meta':
                content = attrs_dict.get('content', '').strip()
                if not content:
                    return
                
                # Capture Open Graph description (prioritized for social media)
                if attrs_dict.get('property', '').lower() == 'og:description' and self.og_description is None:
                    self.og_description = content
                
                # Capture standard meta description as fallback
                elif attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower() == 'description' and self.meta_description is None:
                    self.meta_description = content
        
        def handle_data(self, data):
            if self.in_title:
                self.title_parts.append(data)
        
        def handle_endtag(self, tag):
            if tag.lower() == 'title' and self.in_title:
                self.in_title = False
                if self.title is None:  # Only process first title tag
                    title_text = ''.join(self.title_parts).strip()
                    if title_text:
                        self.title = title_text
    
    # Parse HTML and extract metadata
    parser = MetadataParser()
    parser.feed(html_text)
    parser.close()
    
    # Determine final values with fallbacks
    title = parser.title if parser.title is not None else ''
    description = (
        parser.og_description if parser.og_description is not None 
        else parser.meta_description if parser.meta_description is not None 
        else ''
    )
    
    return {'title': title, 'description': description}
